si.t: Description of work to be done
Coordinate internal and external teams. Track milestones. Report on progress. Manage stakeholders. Report
Reconcile and track time sheets and budgets
1. Kickoff meeting and technical review between key technical partners
Procure lab testing hardware & development kit.

Purchase and set up equipment for testing communication implementations (likely Trialog ComboS). Outcome - project initiated, standards implementation confidence, confident you can progress - testing validity of progressing at that point
Design for new SoC to support 6-channel FCAS metering & disjunctive optimisation
Supports the enhanced metering, and facilitate the upgrade to OCPP2.1
Agreeing with BEV that the standard will be implemented in a consistent way. - Progress report and lessons learnt on standards readiness, implementation and bi-directional charging feasibility
Reposit and EVSE (bi directional charger) agreement that OCPP2.1 will be implemented consistently
Procure EVSE unit for lab testing
Reposit to procure the target EVSE for installation in their lab environment
Develop configuration tools & database structure for EVSE & Vehicle configuration
Build system to identify, store, and record the BEV
Agreement on the implementation of security certificate for BEV OEM (car supplier)and EVSE (car charger)
Engage with DNSPs on connection approval requirements
Confirm DNSP bidirectional charger application process and requirements
Develop bidirectional charging capability within the Reposit controller
Identify existing Reposit customers with potentially compatible vehicles
Potential customer identification within reposit existing customers - requires that they have eligible cars
OCPP 2.1 server implemented on Reposit Controller
Standard implemented on Car charger. (- can a reposit controller send charge and discharge signals, and receive state of charge in test environment)
Test vehicle and EVSE ISO 15118-20 Communication
Test standard implemented successfully. ("- Progress report, lessons learnt and success of hardware upgrade implementations ANd necessary updates on initially agreed specifications")
Reposit controller capable of sending car charger charge or discharge signals, and receive state of charge from BEV
Test end-to-end telemetry from Reposit controller to BEV (notional - actual or mock BEV device)
Identify test vehicle (BYD provided, lent or acquired)
Determine how Reposit will access an appropriate BEV to complete demonstration test in Milestone 3
Vehicle OEM provides test vehicle(s) and/or firmware version for testing
Acquire vehicle for testing. - using test vehicle to ensure that the system is fully operational, standards are implemented and functional, and reposit system and app are ready for BEV customers
Test end-to-end power setpoint response from controller through to vehicle

Test of system response time, which will inform what grid and system services can be delivered.
- Preparing the technology for the demonstration project
Draft customer value proposition for initial No Bill product
Develop product for the customer offering
Test notional product with prospective customers to validate uptake potential
Socialise product offering with customers and determine attractiveness
Add key controls (i.e overrides) and data displays to Reposit app
Enhance existing Reposit app to include key BEV features, i.e override and BEV data displays
Finalise upgraded controller specifications and manufacture prototypes
Identify FCAS services which can be delivered via V2G
Enhance frequency response control to enable V2G FCAS delivery
Upgrade reposit controller to enable FCAS response from BEV
Develop EV forecast engine (predict connectivity, energy needed for mobility)
Build a forecast engine to be able to predict BEV availability and state of charge
Implement new vehicle & EVSE metric collection and publication to cloud
Develop ability to collect information from EVSE (Car charger) and BEV (car) and store on Reposit's cloud
End-to-end test from Reposit App, through to test vehicle
Full test of end to end system functionality on test vehicle
Vehicle OEM produces warranty terms or waivers specifically covering vehicles used within the project
- Acquiring 5 customers (Gen 1), get warranty waivers, develop and execute customer terms and conditions, de
Finalise customer V2G product within project
Release updated Reposit App with EVSE+Vehicle support
Update Reposit App to enable BEV integration
Learning Centre expansion & Customer Support Training
Develop and publish educational articles for Deposits learning center, and support guides for customer support team
Produce installation instructions, safety guidelines and other key material for installers
Develop how-to guides for installers to ensure compliance and full functionality
Purchase EVSE units for customer installs
Create and deploy marketing to identify customers for trial
Identify minimum of 5 customers to participate in the demonstration project (Gen1)
Get DNSP approvals for Gen1 customer installations
Gain DNSP connection approval for bidirectional chargers
Identify first 1 or 2 flagship install sites, and install with oversight and review

Complete installations at initial Gen 1 sites (1 or 2), with heavy oversight to ensure installation is compliance and correct.
Initialise and finalise preparation for demonstration trial
Proceed with installation at rest of Gen1 customer sites
Complete remaining installations using any learnings from the initial installation sites
Use app feedback system and interviews to gather customer experience and sentiment data
After installation is complete, and before cars are utilised, gather customer feedback via social research, and in-app features including override button and real time feedback
Internal review of PKI identification and security
Verify security certificate system is functional
Pass AEMO performance validations, register Gen1 V2G EVSE into FCAS Dispatchable Unit
Enable enrollment in AEMO systems for FCAS
Enable 5-minute spot price response on Gen1 sites

Ensure that the Gen1 sites are capable to participate in 5-minute spot price trading.
Conduct demonstration trial
Enable dynamic network price response where supported on Gen1 sites.
Where a customer DNSP supports Dynamic Network Prices (i.e Project Edith), V2G chargers will begin responding to these.
Testing to verify the robust implementation of cyber security measures
Add Gen1 customers to RERT and/or MSL DUIDs
Ensuring Gen 1 customers can participate in RERT and Minimum system demand events
Develop & finalise experimental design for validating V2G No Bill product
Develop a 6 month demonstration project trial plan
Gather operational data from Gen1 sites over 6 months
Implement the 6 month trial plan - operational of BEV to test response, availability and customer sentiment
Produce key data pack (raw data points, overviews of market participation)
Collect and store demonstration project data

Collect and compile customer experience feedback during and at conclusion of the project.
Data from trial, customer journey and experience, performance reporting, cyber security reporting, lessons learnt and recommendations, including investment decision
Reposit to reach determination on ongoing No Bill V2G offering
Reposit to make investment decision on full product offering of No Bill V2G.
Digital Trust Report, including pentesting results
Compile cyber security report, including recommendations
Produce recommendations report on lessons learnt and advice to industry
Compile lessons learnt and recommendations report
Learning Centre content about V2G, V2G in No Bill, V2G EVSE unit and install info, impacts on specific cars [warranty, safety, app, etc, etc] - Comms person - $1k/day - 10 days
Customer Journey design to include the the V2G product option - Service Designer - $2k/day - 5 days
Customer Journey implementation and testing to include the the V2G product option - Revenue Ops Engineer - $1k/day - 5 days
Customer App changes to include V2G storage - Senior Developer - $1.5k/day - 20 days
Fleet management system changes to incorporate V2G EVSE + Car Models - $1.5k/day - 15 days
Customers to pay the bulk, but we probably need to subsidise a bit, and at least 1 or two of these will be "lab" installs, so not customer-bought. Will be between 30 and 50 in the end
Not currently available/being homologated for Australia, but possibly available when installs commence. Price is a guesstimate. Preferred to the single phase for FCAS performance.
In case we do need to test on a non-consumer vehicle, we'll likely pick up an Atto 1 for $25k incl all costs
No idea what Versinetic will charge.
for Reposits learning center, and support guides for customer support team
Scoping, planning retro, recalibrations. Unknown unknowns
Design for three additional FCAS-compliant metering channels
NOPAT - 7 year losses carried so irrelevent
Window of expected use per day
Available trading energy per trading day
Export rate per day ( net off refill; arbitrage)
VOLL price ( market cap price - what can we rely on, what do we model here?
Quarterly growth rate net ( inludes any attrition)
1 in four cars wont be plugged in during a VOLL event
I would roll this up into Task Cost
Lessons learnt on partner perspectives, expected challenges
2. Develop program dispatch and control technical specification
Description of OCPP2.1 and discussion on localisation recommendations
5. Develop program value computation strategy and technical requirements
(How much of this is IP - commercial in confidence)
Progress Report - Lessons learnt, challenges, issues, customer in
6. Develop and test DERM technical solution
Progress Report - Lesson learnt and test results
6. Operate and Maintain DERM technical solution
8. Develop conceptual customer offering in lieu of BESS at site
9. Undertake detailed economic study of value potentials (dependent on 1/3 of field trials being completed for existing customers)
High level revenue forefast prediction Market analysis
11. Prepare stage 2 trial customer agreement materials
13. Content development about V2G, V2G in No Bill, V2G EVSE unit and install info, impacts on specific cars (warranty, safety, app, etc, etc)
14. Customer Journey design to include the the V2G product option
15. Customer Journey implementation and testing to include the the V2G product option
16. Customer App changes to include V2G storage
17. Fleet management system changes to incorporate V2G EVSE + Car Models
18. Go to market, acquire 10 V2G-only customers
19. Conduct research on protocol learnings for interoperable DERMs in V2G contexts
20. Prepare and release report to OCA
21. Prepare and release report to CharIN
22. Prepare and release report to Standards Australia
23. Finalise report on MQTT vs OCPP control
27. Order and delivery of V2G EVSEs
28. Installation of first 20 EVSEs to existing customers
29. Installation of last 10 EVSEs to new customers
32. VPP live on EV OEM 1 vehicles - existing customers
33. VPP live on EV OEM 1 vehicles - new customers
35. VPP live on EV OEM 2 vehicles - existing customers
36. VPP live on EV OEM 2 vehicles - new customers
38. VPP live on EV OEM 3 vehicles - existing customers
39. VPP live on EV OEM 3 vehicles - new customers
41. VPP live on EV OEM 4 vehicles - existing customers
42. VPP live on EV OEM 4 vehicles - new customers
44. VPP live on EV OEM 5 vehicles - existing customers
45. VPP live on EV OEM 5 vehicles - new customers
48. Return phase 1 OEM 1 vehicles to OEM
49. Return phase 2 OEM 1 vehicles to OEM
50. Transfer phase 1 OEM 1 customers to production code, remove any trial infrastructure
51. Transfer phase 2 OEM 1 customers to production code, remove any trial infrastructure
53. Return phase 1 OEM 2 vehicles to OEM
54. Return phase 2 OEM 2 vehicles to OEM
55. Transfer phase 1 OEM 2 customers to production code, remove any trial infrastructure
56. Transfer phase 2 OEM 2 customers to production code, remove any trial infrastructure
58. Return phase 1 OEM 3 vehicles to OEM
59. Return phase 2 OEM 3 vehicles to OEM
60. Transfer phase 1 OEM 3 customers to production code, remove any trial infrastructure
61. Transfer phase 2 OEM 3 customers to production code, remove any trial infrastructure
63. Return phase 1 OEM 4 vehicles to OEM
64. Return phase 2 OEM 4 vehicles to OEM
65. Transfer phase 1 OEM 4 customers to production code, remove any trial infrastructure
66. Transfer phase 2 OEM 4 customers to production code, remove any trial infrastructure
68. Return phase 1 OEM 5 vehicles to OEM
69. Return phase 2 OEM 5 vehicles to OEM
70. Transfer phase 1 OEM 5 customers to production code, remove any trial infrastructure
71. Transfer phase 2 OEM 5 customers to production code, remove any trial infrastructure
1. Development of V2G PKI performance targets against Australian and international cybersecurity landscape (internal report for ENA)
2. Consultation on V2G PKI performance targets
3. Final reporting on V2G PKI performance targets
4. Development of private PKI / test environment processes for ISO 15118-2
5. Development of private PKI / test environment processes for ISO 15118-20
6. Development of processes to provision and use Hubject APAC PKI in ISO 15118-2 secured charging
7. Development of processes to provision and use Hubject APAC PKI in ISO 15118-20 secured charging
13. Development of report detailing PKI processes for secured charging in Australia under ISO 15118-2 and ISO 15118-20
14. Consultation on report detailing PKI processes for secured charging in Australia under ISO 15118-2 and ISO 15118-20
15. Finalisation of report detailing PKI processes for secured charging in Australia under ISO 15118-2 and ISO 15118-20
16. Publishing of report detailing PKI processes for secured charging in Australia under ISO 15118-2 and ISO 15118-20
17. Study of proliferation, scalability and implementation requirements of ISO 15118 PKI ecosystems in Australia
18. Review of work, key systems and components inherent throughout the project compiled in the context of a study of knowledge, standards gaps and value transferability study for AC V2G
